14.reason: Error: The condition `s[i+1]<='9'` does not check that `s[i-1]` is a digit; it only checks the lower bound. Should be `s[i-1]>='0'&&s[i-1]<='9'&&s[i+1]>='0'&&s[i+1]<='9'&&s[i+1]>s[i-1]`. Also, when i=0, s[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory.

Also, the condition s[i+1]<='9' is correct but the lower bound s[i+1]>='0' is missing. This can cause incorrect expansion when characters are outside the digit range.

17.reason: Loop prints from s[i-1] inclusive, causing duplicate character; should start from j=1

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Loop prints from s[i-1] inclusive, causing duplicate character; should start from j=1
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Loop should start from j=1 to avoid printing the starting character again (the start character is printed by the else branch)
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Incorrect loop bounds: starting at 0 duplicates s[i-1] and stopping at lenth-1 omits s[i+1]. Should be j=1;j<lenth;j to print only intermediate characters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Loop starts at 0, causing the character before '-' to be printed again; should start at 1.
